title: Relay Pumping: LDH Supply to Attack Engine

scene.description: Engine 181 is at the hydrant supplying Engine 183 through 800 feet of 4" LDH. Engine 183 needs 500 GPM delivered. The relay lay has a 30-foot elevation gain from Engine 181 to Engine 183. Engine 183 needs 20 PSI residual intake pressure. What discharge pressure should Engine 181 pump?

problems.title: Relay Pumping: LDH Supply to Attack Engine
problems.question: Engine 181 is at the hydrant supplying Engine 183 through 800 feet of 4" LDH. Engine 183 needs 500 GPM delivered. The relay lay has a 30-foot elevation gain from Engine 181 to Engine 183. Engine 183 needs 20 PSI residual intake pressure. What discharge pressure should Engine 181 pump?
problems.inputLabel: Your relay pump discharge pressure answer
formulaBreakdown: Relay Pumping PDP = friction loss + elevation pressure + desired residual intake pressure.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds of feet.
Desired residual intake pressure at Engine 183 = 20 PSI.
Nozzle pressure is 0 because Engine 181 is supplying the receiving engine, not the nozzle.
problems.instructorExplanation: This is a relay supply problem. Engine 181 only needs to overcome LDH friction loss, elevation gain, and maintain the desired intake pressure at Engine 183.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes: adding nozzle pressure, forgetting residual intake pressure, forgetting elevation gain, using the wrong C-value for 4" LDH, or calculating friction loss for only 100 feet instead of 800 feet.
